text: Periodized carbohydrate restriction does not enhance endurance performance compared to training with normal high carbohydrate availability.
population: trained endurance athletes (VO2max ≥55 ml/kg/min females, ≥60 ml/kg/min males)

text: Training or recovering with low muscle glycogen stimulates mitochondrial biogenesis and favorable metabolic adaptations, but these cellular benefits do not translate to measurable performance gains.
Chronic carbohydrate restriction compromises training quality, particularly reducing intensity during peak intervals.
Endurance athletes should maintain carbohydrate-rich diets for optimal performance during competitions and intense training sessions.
If using train-low strategies, they should be applied only to selected low-intensity sessions rather than as chronic carbohydrate restriction across all training.
specific_value: ≥3 sessions per week of restriction studied
